pdg_node.informal_desc: Converts physiological time constants (ms) and algorithm tuning ratios into integer sample counts based on the sampling rate, and initialises scalar thresholds used throughout detection.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Applies a 4-sample lag finite-difference differentiator to the raw ECG signal, producing a first-order difference series that accentuates rapid amplitude changes associated with QRS complexes.

declaration.name: differentiate_signal
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float list
declaration.docstring: Applies a 4-sample lag finite-difference differentiator to the raw ECG signal, producing a first-order difference series that accentuates rapid amplitude changes associated with QRS complexes.

declaration.name: differentiate_signal
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float list
declaration.docstring: Applies a 4-sample lag finite-difference differentiator to the raw ECG signal, producing a first-order difference series that accentuates rapid amplitude changes associated with QRS complexes.

declaration.name: differentiate_signal
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float list
declaration.docstring: Applies a 4-sample lag finite-difference differentiator to the raw ECG signal, producing a first-order difference series that accentuates rapid amplitude changes associated with QRS complexes.

pdg_node.statement: (y1: 1-D float list) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, int]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Convolves the differentiated series y1 with the fixed 10-tap symmetric FIR kernel [1,4,6,4,1,-1,-4,-6,-4,-1], yielding the band-limited transformed signal y2 used for threshold crossing detection.

declaration.name: low_pass_filter_transform
declaration.type_signature: (y1: 1-D float list) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, int]
declaration.docstring: Convolves the differentiated series y1 with the fixed 10-tap symmetric FIR kernel [1,4,6,4,1,-1,-4,-6,-4,-1], yielding the band-limited transformed signal y2 used for threshold crossing detection.

pdg_node.statement: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Miterate: int (samples), mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, float, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Seeds the adaptive upper (MM / Th) and lower (NN / ThNew) threshold arrays from the extrema of y2 over the first Miterate samples, providing starting references for the iterative QRS detector.

declaration.name: initialise_adaptive_thresholds
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Miterate: int (samples), mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, float, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float]
declaration.docstring: Seeds the adaptive upper (MM / Th) and lower (NN / ThNew) threshold arrays from the extrema of y2 over the first Miterate samples, providing starting references for the iterative QRS detector.

declaration.name: initialise_adaptive_thresholds
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Miterate: int (samples), mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, float, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float]
declaration.docstring: Seeds the adaptive upper (MM / Th) and lower (NN / ThNew) threshold arrays from the extrema of y2 over the first Miterate samples, providing starting references for the iterative QRS detector.

declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Miterate: int (samples), mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, float, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float]

pdg_node.statement: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, y2_len: int, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), Miterate: int (samples), v1500ms: int (samples), MM: 1-D float numpy array, length 3, MMidx: int, NN: 1-D float numpy array, length 2, NNidx: int, mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float, float, int]
pdg_node.informal_desc: At each changeM-sample window boundary, recomputes the local extrema of y2 in the new window, clips runaway growth (cap at 1.5x previous value), and updates the rolling MM / NN buffers plus their scalar means Th / ThNew.

declaration.name: update_adaptive_thresholds
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, y2_len: int, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), Miterate: int (samples), v1500ms: int (samples), MM: 1-D float numpy array, length 3, MMidx: int, NN: 1-D float numpy array, length 2, NNidx: int, mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float, float, int]
declaration.docstring: At each changeM-sample window boundary, recomputes the local extrema of y2 in the new window, clips runaway growth (cap at 1.5x previous value), and updates the rolling MM / NN buffers plus their scalar means Th / ThNew.

declaration.name: update_adaptive_thresholds
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, y2_len: int, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), Miterate: int (samples), v1500ms: int (samples), MM: 1-D float numpy array, length 3, MMidx: int, NN: 1-D float numpy array, length 2, NNidx: int, mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float, float, int]
declaration.docstring: At each changeM-sample window boundary, recomputes the local extrema of y2 in the new window, clips runaway growth (cap at 1.5x previous value), and updates the rolling MM / NN buffers plus their scalar means Th / ThNew.

declaration.name: update_adaptive_thresholds
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, y2_len: int, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), Miterate: int (samples), v1500ms: int (samples), MM: 1-D float numpy array, length 3, MMidx: int, NN: 1-D float numpy array, length 2, NNidx: int, mmth: float, mmp: float) -> tuple[1-D float numpy array, length 3, int, 1-D float numpy array, length 2, int, float, float, int]
declaration.docstring: At each changeM-sample window boundary, recomputes the local extrema of y2 in the new window, clips runaway growth (cap at 1.5x previous value), and updates the rolling MM / NN buffers plus their scalar means Th / ThNew.

pdg_node.statement: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Th: float, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), err_kill: int (samples), nthfpluss: list of int, rpeaks: list of int, v250ms: int (samples), v1200ms: int (samples)) -> tuple[int (global sample index), bool, bool]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Locates the next positive-going threshold crossing (nthf+) in the current changeM-wide window of y2 by intersecting indices where y2 > Th with those where the next sample y2 < Th, returning the crossing index adjusted to the global timeline.

declaration.name: find_threshold_crossing
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Th: float, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), err_kill: int (samples), nthfpluss: list of int, rpeaks: list of int, v250ms: int (samples), v1200ms: int (samples)) -> tuple[int (global sample index), bool, bool]
declaration.docstring: Locates the next positive-going threshold crossing (nthf+) in the current changeM-wide window of y2 by intersecting indices where y2 > Th with those where the next sample y2 < Th, returning the crossing index adjusted to the global timeline.

declaration.name: find_threshold_crossing
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Th: float, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), err_kill: int (samples), nthfpluss: list of int, rpeaks: list of int, v250ms: int (samples), v1200ms: int (samples)) -> tuple[int (global sample index), bool, bool]
declaration.docstring: Locates the next positive-going threshold crossing (nthf+) in the current changeM-wide window of y2 by intersecting indices where y2 > Th with those where the next sample y2 < Th, returning the crossing index adjusted to the global timeline.

declaration.name: find_threshold_crossing
declaration.type_signature: (y2: 1-D float numpy array, Th: float, inc: int, changeM: int (samples), err_kill: int (samples), nthfpluss: list of int, rpeaks: list of int, v250ms: int (samples), v1200ms: int (samples)) -> tuple[int (global sample index), bool, bool]
declaration.docstring: Locates the next positive-going threshold crossing (nthf+) in the current changeM-wide window of y2 by intersecting indices where y2 > Th with those where the next sample y2 < Th, returning the crossing index adjusted to the global timeline.

pdg_node.statement: (signal: 1-D float array, y2: 1-D float numpy array, nthfplus: int (global sample index), ThNew: float, v180ms: int (samples), p10ms: int (samples), p20ms: int (samples), y2_len: int) -> int or None
pdg_node.informal_desc: Given a confirmed threshold crossing, searches the subsequent 180 ms window for a run of at least p10ms consecutive samples below ThNew (hold condition), then pinpoints the R-peak as the maximum of the raw signal within that window (with a small rightward look-ahead of p20ms).

declaration.name: detect_rpeak_in_window
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array, y2: 1-D float numpy array, nthfplus: int (global sample index), ThNew: float, v180ms: int (samples), p10ms: int (samples), p20ms: int (samples), y2_len: int) -> int or None
declaration.docstring: Given a confirmed threshold crossing, searches the subsequent 180 ms window for a run of at least p10ms consecutive samples below ThNew (hold condition), then pinpoints the R-peak as the maximum of the raw signal within that window (with a small rightward look-ahead of p20ms).

declaration.name: detect_rpeak_in_window
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array, y2: 1-D float numpy array, nthfplus: int (global sample index), ThNew: float, v180ms: int (samples), p10ms: int (samples), p20ms: int (samples), y2_len: int) -> int or None
declaration.docstring: Given a confirmed threshold crossing, searches the subsequent 180 ms window for a run of at least p10ms consecutive samples below ThNew (hold condition), then pinpoints the R-peak as the maximum of the raw signal within that window (with a small rightward look-ahead of p20ms).

declaration.name: detect_rpeak_in_window
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array, y2: 1-D float numpy array, nthfplus: int (global sample index), ThNew: float, v180ms: int (samples), p10ms: int (samples), p20ms: int (samples), y2_len: int) -> int or None
declaration.docstring: Given a confirmed threshold crossing, searches the subsequent 180 ms window for a run of at least p10ms consecutive samples below ThNew (hold condition), then pinpoints the R-peak as the maximum of the raw signal within that window (with a small rightward look-ahead of p20ms).

pdg_node.statement: (rpeaks: list of int) -> 1-D int numpy array
pdg_node.informal_desc: Removes duplicate R-peak indices accumulated across all windows, sorts them in ascending order, and converts the result to a typed integer NumPy array ready for output.

declaration.name: deduplicate_and_sort_rpeaks
declaration.type_signature: (rpeaks: list of int) -> 1-D int numpy array
declaration.docstring: Removes duplicate R-peak indices accumulated across all windows, sorts them in ascending order, and converts the result to a typed integer NumPy array ready for output.
